content:
AUTONOMOUS IA OPERATIONS DASHBOARD — AUDIT EXISTING SYSTEM AND COMPLETE NOW

I need full visibility into what IVX Autonomous does every day.

First, audit the existing codebase and database to determine whether an Autonomous Operations Dashboard already exists.

Search for:

- autonomous dashboard
- AI agents dashboard
- agent activity
- task execution
- daily reports
- agent logs
- execution logs
- workflow history
- task queue
- investor reports
- code reports
- deployment reports
- lead generation reports
- opportunity scanner
- CRM agent activity
- developer agent activity
- notification history
- audit ledger
- proof ledger
- agent performance
- agent metrics
- 12 AI agents
- daily executive report

DO NOT immediately create a duplicate dashboard.

==================================================
1. RETURN EXISTING DASHBOARD AUDIT FIRST
==================================================

Return:

ITEM | EXISTS YES/NO | ROUTE | FILES | DATABASE TABLE | LIVE URL | WORKING YES/NO

Include:

1. Owner Autonomous Dashboard
2. AI Agent Activity Dashboard
3. Daily Report Dashboard
4. Task Queue
5. Task History
6. Agent Execution Logs
7. Code and Deployment Activity
8. Investor Activity
9. Buyer Activity
10. Lead Generation Activity
11. CRM Activity
12. Property Activity
13. Reels and Media Activity
14. Landing Page Activity
15. Member Registration Activity
16. Revenue Activity
17. Error and Failure Activity
18. Owner Action Requests
19. Push Notification History
20. Proof and Evidence Ledger

If a dashboard already exists, repair and complete it instead of creating another one.

==================================================
2. REQUIRED OWNER DASHBOARD
==================================================

Create or complete one Owner-only dashboard:

OWNER HQ
→ AUTONOMOUS OPERATIONS
→ DAILY IA REPORT

The owner must be able to see items numbered from 1 to 100 or more.

Required views:

- Today
- Yesterday
- Last 7 days
- Last 30 days
- Custom date range
- Live activity
- By AI agent
- By department
- By task status
- By business category
- By repository
- By deployment
- By investor
- By property
- By lead source

==================================================
3. 12 AI AGENT BREAKDOWN
==================================================

Show all 12 AI agents separately.

For each agent display:

- Agent number
- Agent name
- Department
- Primary responsibility
- Current status
- Current task
- Tasks started today
- Tasks completed today
- Tasks failed today
- Tasks blocked today
- Code files changed
- Commits created
- Deployments completed
- APIs tested
- Errors fixed
- Investors processed
- Buyers processed
- Leads generated
- Messages sent
- Reports created
- Revenue opportunity identified
- Owner action required
- Last activity time
- Total execution time
- Success rate
- Evidence link
- Trace ID

Required statuses:

- ACTIVE
- IDLE
- RUNNING
- TESTING
- DEPLOYING
- VERIFYING
- RETRYING
- BLOCKED
- OWNER ACTION REQUIRED
- FAILED
- COMPLETED

Do not show “ACTIVE” unless a real task is currently executing.

==================================================
4. DAILY REPORT ITEMS 1–100+
==================================================

Every daily report must contain a numbered activity list.

Example structure:

1. Agent
2. Department
3. Category
4. Task
5. Action executed
6. Result
7. Status
8. Start time
9. End time
10. Duration
11. Repository
12. Branch
13. Commit SHA
14. Deployment ID
15. Production URL
16. Investor ID
17. Property ID
18. Lead ID
19. Error
20. Retry count
21. Evidence

The owner must be able to scroll through:

- Item 1
- Item 2
- Item 3
- …
- Item 100
- More than 100 when available

Do not summarize 100 actions into one generic paragraph.

Every activity must be a real stored record.

==================================================
5. BREAKDOWN BY CATEGORY
==================================================

Create categories for:

A. DEVELOPMENT

- Code audit
- Bug fixes
- Frontend
- Backend
- Mobile
- Expo
- Android
- iOS
- APIs
- Database
- Supabase
- Security
- Testing
- Deployment
- Monitoring
- Reels
- Chat
- Landing page
- Owner login

B. INVESTORS

- New investors
- Investor onboarding
- Investor follow-up
- Investor verification
- Investor interests
- Investor transactions
- Investor statements
- Investor opportunities
- Investor messages
- Investor status changes

C. BUYERS

- New buyers
- Buyer preferences
- Buyer location
- Buyer budget
- Buyer matching
- Buyer outreach
- Buyer responses
- Buyer conversion

D. LEADS AND CRM

- Leads found
- Leads verified
- Duplicate leads removed
- Leads contacted
- Responses received
- Follow-ups
- Appointments
- Conversions
- Lead score changes

E. PROPERTIES AND DEALS

- Properties added
- Properties updated
- Deal status
- Property media
- Reels
- Investor count
- Buyer matches
- Financial updates
- Documents
- Due diligence

F. MARKETING

- Landing page
- Registration
- Ads
- Social media
- Influencers
- Reels
- Engagement
- Traffic
- Conversion
- Campaign results

G. FINANCIAL

- Money received
- Money paid
- Investor balances
- Realtor payments
- Influencer commissions
- Profit
- Loss
- Revenue
- Fees
- Pending transactions

H. AUTONOMOUS SYSTEM

- Tasks created
- Tasks completed
- Tasks failed
- Retries
- Owner blockers
- Notifications sent
- Agent performance
- Tool performance
- Credential verification
- Self-healing actions

==================================================
6. BREAKDOWN BY INVESTOR
==================================================

Add an investor activity view.

For each investor show:

- Investor name
- Investor ID
- Assigned AI agent
- Last activity
- Properties viewed
- Deals reviewed
- Messages sent
- Messages received
- Documents requested
- Documents completed
- KYC status
- Investment interest
- Investment amount
- Transactions
- Follow-up status
- Next autonomous action
- Blocker
- Full activity history

Never expose one investor’s private data to another investor.

Owner-only access.

==================================================
7. CODE AND DEPLOYMENT REPORT
==================================================

Show all development work by day.

Required fields:

- AI agent
- Task
- Repository
- Branch
- Files changed
- Lines added
- Lines removed
- Commit SHA
- Pull request
- Test result
- Build result
- Deployment provider
- Deployment ID
- Deployment status
- Production SHA
- GitHub SHA match
- Production URL
- Rollback status
- Evidence
- Error
- Retry count

A code change without a commit must show:

UNCOMMITTED

A commit without deployment must show:

NOT DEPLOYED

A deployment without live verification must show:

NOT VERIFIED

==================================================
8. LIVE AUTONOMOUS ACTIVITY FEED
==================================================

Create a live activity feed showing:

- time
- agent
- department
- current action
- status
- progress percentage
- trace ID
- task ID

Examples:

10:04 AM — Developer Agent — Testing /api/reels — RUNNING  
10:06 AM — Investor Agent — Verified 14 investor records — COMPLETED  
10:08 AM — Deployment Agent — Render deployment failed — RETRYING  
10:11 AM — CRM Agent — Added 27 qualified buyers — COMPLETED  

Do not display fake or simulated activity.

==================================================
9. DAILY EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
==================================================

Generate one owner report every day containing:

- Total tasks started
- Total tasks completed
- Total tasks failed
- Total tasks blocked
- Total retries
- Total deployments
- Total code commits
- Total bugs fixed
- Total investors processed
- Total buyers processed
- Total leads generated
- Total properties updated
- Total messages sent
- Total revenue opportunities
- Total owner actions required
- 12-agent utilization
- Top completed work
- Top failures
- Business risks
- Next 24-hour plan

Also include:

- Yesterday versus today
- 7-day trend
- 30-day trend
- Agent-by-agent comparison
- Department-by-department comparison

==================================================
10. DATA MODEL
==================================================

Use or create the minimum required tables:

autonomous_agents
autonomous_tasks
autonomous_task_steps
autonomous_activity
autonomous_daily_reports
autonomous_notifications
autonomous_evidence
autonomous_agent_metrics
autonomous_owner_actions

Each activity record must include:

- id
- task_id
- agent_id
- agent_name
- department
- category
- action_type
- description
- status
- progress
- repository
- branch
- commit_sha
- deployment_id
- investor_id
- buyer_id
- property_id
- lead_id
- trace_id
- evidence_url
- error_code
- error_message
- retry_count
- started_at
- completed_at
- created_at
- updated_at

Do not store passwords, tokens or private secrets in activity logs.

==================================================
11. OWNER NOTIFICATIONS
==================================================

Send push/chat notifications only for:

- Critical task failure
- Owner action required
- Deployment completed
- Deployment failed after retries
- Security issue
- Investor urgent action
- Large financial event
- Daily report ready

Every notification must deep-link to the exact dashboard item.

==================================================
12. OWNER CONTROLS
==================================================

Owner must be able to:

- Open an agent
- View all agent tasks
- Pause an agent
- Resume an agent
- Reassign a task
- Change priority
- Cancel a task
- Retry a failed task
- Open evidence
- Open commit
- Open deployment
- Open investor
- Open property
- Request a new report
- Export report to PDF
- Export report to CSV
- Filter by date, agent and category

Dangerous actions must require owner confirmation.

==================================================
13. HONEST REPORTING RULE
==================================================

Never fabricate autonomous activity.

Every completed item must have at least one real evidence source:

- database record
- commit SHA
- deployment ID
- API result
- test result
- investor action
- CRM record
- screenshot
- execution log

If no evidence exists, show:

UNVERIFIED

If an agent is not executing, show:

IDLE

If the system cannot identify 12 real agents, show exactly how many real agents exist and which claimed agents are only labels or placeholders.

==================================================
14. FINAL REQUIRED RESPONSE
==================================================

Return:

A. EXISTING SYSTEM AUDIT

- Existing dashboard: YES/NO
- Existing live route
- Existing files
- Existing database tables
- Existing 12 agents
- Working parts
- Broken parts
- Missing parts

B. IMPLEMENTATION RESULT

- Dashboard URL
- Owner route
- GitHub SHA
- Deployment ID
- Database migrations
- Number of real agents
- Number of tasks today
- Number completed
- Number failed
- Number blocked
- Daily report evidence
- Push notification evidence

C. SCREENSHOTS

- Main autonomous dashboard
- 12-agent view
- Daily report 1–100
- Code report
- Investor report
- Live activity feed
- Owner action request
- Mobile Android view
- Desktop view

==================================================
FINAL STATUS RULE
==================================================

Do not report COMPLETE until:

1. The dashboard is deployed live.
2. Owner access works.
3. All real AI agents are visible.
4. Daily items 1–100 are visible.
5. Code work is traceable to commits and deployments.
6. Investor activity is visible by investor.
7. Reports can be filtered by category.
8. Push notifications open the correct activity.
9. Fake or unsupported activity is not displayed.
10. The owner can see exactly what autonomous did today.

FIRST ACTION:

Audit whether this dashboard already exists.

Do not create a duplicate until the existing dashboard audit is complete.
